title: melicope littoralis
intro: Melicope littoralis, also known as Coastal Melicope or Leptodermis littoralis, is a small to medium-sized tree or shrub native to the coastal regions of the Pacific Islands. It is a member of the Rutaceae family, which includes citrus and rue plants. The medicinal properties of Melicope littoralis include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions, and it can be prepared as a tea or decoction for consumption, or as a topical application for skin issues.
